title: Phase A: HeroUI setup + theme token remap
description: Verify Pro auth/resolution, remap HeroUI theme tokens onto existing coral OKLCH palette in globals.css, smoke-test a Button build. Part of blog-zs8.
close_reason: HeroUI setup verified; theme remapped; smoke passed

title: Migrate shadcn UI to HeroUI (OSS + Pro)
description: Replace all shadcn base-maia primitives in src/components/ui and hand-rolled raw HTML markup with HeroUI OSS/Pro components. Preserve coral OKLCH look, migrate recharts to HeroUI Pro charts, delete shadcn at end. Incremental by phase. Plan: ~/.claude/plans/migrate-shadcn-heroui-twinkly-penguin.md
close_reason: shadcn fully removed: deleted src/components/ui (24 files), components.json, dead shadcn/tailwind.css import; removed shadcn/@base-ui/react/recharts/react-resizable-panels deps (kept hugeicons/cva/clsx/tailwind-merge/tailwind-variants). Build 65/65 pages, typecheck+lint clean. Migration complete.

title: Add PostHog tracking alongside Umami
description: Add PostHog client-side pageview tracking through the Vercel PostHog integration while keeping existing Umami and Vercel Analytics active.
acceptance_criteria: PostHog provider is wired with Vercel integration env vars; Umami script remains active; Vercel Analytics remains active; dashboard source remains Umami during warm-up.

title: Set up HeroUI Pro foundation
description: Add and configure the HeroUI and HeroUI Pro foundation needed for the blog redesign. Use the Default preset as the target, preserve the correct CSS import order, and avoid activating Brutalism or Glass globally. This work should document how HeroUI Pro coexists with shadcn/ui while shadcn remains legacy during migration.
acceptance_criteria: @heroui/react and @heroui-pro/react are available; CSS imports follow HeroUI Pro requirements; no global Brutalism or Glass theme is enabled; project documentation or implementation notes explain the temporary coexistence with shadcn/ui.

title: Create Blog DESIGN.md
description: Define the public blog design direction using the google-labs-code DESIGN.md format. The document should use name: Blog, describe HeroUI Pro Default as the target design system, mark shadcn/ui as legacy during migration, and capture the warm editorial colour direction, typography, layout, components, and do/don't rules for the blog redesign.
acceptance_criteria: DESIGN.md exists at the repo root; it follows the DESIGN.md alpha section order; it uses name: Blog; it identifies HeroUI Pro Default as the target; it treats shadcn/ui as legacy during migration; it includes blog-specific component guidance and do/don't rules.

title: Standardise motion imports on RSC-safe motion/react-client
description: Convert hero-section, featured-work, not-found, and dashboard views-by-page from 'use client' + motion/react to Server Components using motion/react-client, matching latest-posts.tsx. Removes unnecessary client boundaries.

title: Complete Phase F: remove all shadcn remnants
description: Migration left the shadcn foundation in place: token layer + @theme inline bridge in globals.css, cn/cva utilities, dead deps (recharts, react-resizable-panels, class-variance-authority, clsx, tailwind-merge, tw-animate-css), shadcn MCP/skill registration, and stale docs. Re-pin HeroUI tokens to coral/warm-paper, rename shadcn utility classes to HeroUI equivalents, strip deps/config/docs. Zero visual change. Plan: ~/.claude/plans/check-if-any-remnant-starry-owl.md
close_reason: Phase F complete: shadcn foundation removed (tokens re-pinned to HeroUI, cn from @heroui/react, utility classes migrated, deps/config/docs cleaned). recharts/react-resizable-panels/tailwind-merge kept as HeroUI Pro peer deps. typecheck/lint/build green; only pre-existing MCP-route test failures remain.

title: Phase F: Delete shadcn + unused deps
description: Delete src/components/ui, components.json, shadcn/tailwind.css import; remove shadcn/@base-ui/react/recharts/react-resizable-panels deps (KEEP hugeicons). pnpm install + full build/test. Part of blog-zs8.
close_reason: shadcn fully removed: deleted src/components/ui (24 files), components.json, dead shadcn/tailwind.css import; removed shadcn/@base-ui/react/recharts/react-resizable-panels deps (kept hugeicons/cva/clsx/tailwind-merge/tailwind-variants). Build 65/65 pages, typecheck+lint clean. Migration complete.

title: Phase B: Migrate public site (main) to HeroUI
description: Card/badge/button/popover/scroll-area + raw markup (header nav, footer, tag-selector, card-divs, avatars, pills) across blog/about/dashboard. Part of blog-zs8.
close_reason: Public site migrated to HeroUI: Card/Chip/Button/Popover/ScrollShadow + raw markup (button-links via buttonVariants, pills, dividers). Typecheck clean, routes render 200.

title: MCP route test fails: WebStandardStreamableHTTPServerTransport is not a constructor
description: src/app/api/mcp/__tests__/route.test.ts has 2 failing tests (POST/auth paths) due to the mocked WebStandardStreamableHTTPServerTransport not being constructable. Pre-existing on clean main (confirmed via git stash), unrelated to the usage-ingest work. Likely an MCP SDK export/mock shape drift. Fix the vi.mock factory to return a proper constructor.

title: Add authenticated /api/usage/ingest route for prod usage ingestion
description: The usage:ingest script parses LOCAL agent logs and can only run on the local machine, but local env points at the Neon dev branch. Add a POST /api/usage/ingest route that upserts already-aggregated token_usage rows using the deployment's own DATABASE_URL (prod on Vercel), gated by BLOG_MCP_AUTH_TOKEN / admin session. Local script computes rows and POSTs them, so no prod DB credentials ever touch the laptop. Route revalidates the 'usage' cache tag after writing.
close_reason: Implemented /api/usage/ingest route + script remote path; tests/typecheck/lint pass, committed in 00226ff and pushed

title: Add OpenCode support to /usage token-usage profile
description: Add OpenCode (SQLite opencode.db, multi-provider) as a third agent in the usage profile. Read source via node:sqlite; destination stays Neon Postgres token_usage. Requires widening the token_usage PK to (date, agent, provider, model), adding provider to UsageEvent, a per-event provider resolver, an opencode parser, and a pricing signature change to accept explicit provider. Plan: ~/.claude/plans/i-think-i-need-functional-hamming.md
notes: Implemented: opencode SQLite parser (node:sqlite, no new deps), provider?: on UsageEvent, resolveProvider(), pricing now takes {agent,provider}, ingest folds on (date,agent,provider,model), token_usage PK widened to include provider. 14 new unit tests pass; typecheck clean. db:push applied + usage:ingest run: opencode=2.96B tokens across openai/fireworks-ai/opencode-go/opencode/ollama. Verified in DB. Pending: commit+push.
close_reason: Implemented, tested (14 new unit tests), schema pushed, ingested + verified in DB, committed 8fc78a2 and pushed.

title: Add provider dimension to token-usage (derived from agent)
description: Track inference provider (anthropic/openai) as a separate, queryable dimension alongside agent on the /usage feature. Provider is functionally derived from the agent (claude->anthropic, codex->openai) via a single shared source of truth reused by pricing. Additive: agent column/label unchanged; add provider column+index, store it at ingest, add byProvider rollup + providers summary, render a By provider breakdown card.
close_reason: Added provider dimension (derived from agent) across schema, ingest, queries, types and /usage page. db:push applied; re-ingest backfilled 172 rows (anthropic/claude=42, openai/codex=130), no 'unknown' remaining. Typecheck + Biome clean.

title: Migrate Git hooks from Husky to Lefthook
description: Replace Husky 9 + lint-staged with Lefthook. Add lefthook.yml (pre-commit: gitleaks + biome on staged files; commit-msg: commitlint), update package.json prepare script and deps, add pnpm.onlyBuiltDependencies, remove .husky, update README.

title: Remove husky, migrate hooks to lefthook
description: lefthook is now installed. Migrate gitleaks secret scan and commitlint to lefthook, remove husky + orphaned lint-staged, re-point git hooks.
close_reason: Migrated gitleaks + commitlint + ultracite to lefthook.yml, removed husky and orphaned lint-staged, re-pointed git hooks via lefthook install, updated README.

title: Setup Ultracite
description: Adopt Ultracite (opinionated Biome preset) via full ultracite init. Convert biome.json to biome.jsonc extending ultracite/biome core+react+next, adopt Ultracite defaults, wire up agent rules (claude/codex/opencode) and husky/lint-staged.

title: Token mix stacked bar on /usage
description: Add a compact horizontal stacked bar to /usage showing the all-time split of tokens by category (input/output/cacheRead/cacheWrite/reasoning). Data already aggregated per day in token_usage; expose an all-time tokenMix total on UsageProfile and render with HeroUI Pro BarChart. RSC shell + client leaf.
close_reason: Token mix stacked bar shipped: all-time tokenMix on UsageProfile, RSC shell + client BarChart leaf using HeroUI --chart-1..5 tokens, legend per HeroUI docs pattern. typecheck/lint/build green.

title: Build /usage contribution heatmap (KIV — cell component approach undecided)
description: The GitHub-style daily contribution heatmap is the page centrepiece but is on hold. Open question: how to render ~250 day cells given HeroUI-first directive vs dense custom grid — options were (a) HeroUI Button+Tooltip per active cell, (b) lightweight cells + one hover-driven HeroUI Tooltip, (c) hand-rolled SVG + native title. Pure layout helper src/lib/usage/heatmap-layout.ts is already built and ready. DayContribution has per-day totals + intensity (0-4 quantile) + tokenBreakdown + per-agent breakdown for the tooltip. Cost may be null (render N.A.).
close_reason: Implemented hand-rolled CSS-grid contribution heatmap with HeroUI OSS Tooltip per active day; wired into /usage between stats and trend; added unit tests for buildHeatmapLayout

title: Price Codex outlier models via MODEL_ALIASES (codex-auto-review, legacy unknown)
description: codex-auto-review (~27.6M tokens) and legacy v0.34.0 'unknown' (~18.7M tokens) are absent from models.dev/LiteLLM/OpenRouter. Add an agent-scoped MODEL_ALIASES map in src/lib/usage/pricing.ts resolving codex:{codex-auto-review->gpt-5-codex, unknown->gpt-5} for cost only (identical $1.25/$10/$0.125 tier). Parser stays faithful; labels unchanged. Then db:push + usage:ingest to verify non-zero cost.
close_reason: Implemented MODEL_ALIASES (codex-auto-review->gpt-5-codex) + N.A. handling: costOf returns number|null, costUsd nullable. Verified via ingest: codex-auto-review priced $11.29/14 rows, unknown null/N.A./3 rows. Total cost $2202.87, 3.22B tokens, 113 days.

title: Personal token-usage profile page (/usage)
description: Public 'wrapped'-style page showing tokens/cost across AI coding agents (Claude Code + Codex), with GitHub-style SVG heatmap, stat cards, and per-agent/model breakdowns. Local ingest script parses ~/.claude + ~/.codex logs, prices via models.dev, upserts daily aggregates to Neon; page reads via Next.js use cache. See plan file.

description: Update the current Umami-backed visits chart from 30 days to 90 days and make the dashboard label explicit.
acceptance_criteria: Visits chart queries 90 days; chart title mentions 90 days; views by page remains 30 days; total visits remains all-time.

acceptance_criteria: Umami script removed from layout; UMAMI_API_URL, UMAMI_API_TOKEN, and UMAMI_WEBSITE_ID removed from docs and Vercel; Umami token revoked if still active; stale Umami comments and docs cleaned up.

title: Migrate dashboard analytics reads from Umami to PostHog after warm-up

acceptance_criteria: Dashboard total visits, 90-day visits chart, and 30-day views by page read from PostHog; server-side PostHog env vars are documented; imports no longer depend on src/lib/umami.ts.

title: Migrate blog components off shadcn/ui
description: Remove blog-specific imports from @/components/ui/* as part of the migration from shadcn/ui to HeroUI Pro. Focus on components under src/app/(main)/blog and replace shadcn Card, Badge, Popover, ScrollArea, and related primitives with HeroUI or HeroUI Pro equivalents where appropriate.
acceptance_criteria: Blog-specific components no longer import from @/components/ui/*; replacements use HeroUI or HeroUI Pro where appropriate; behaviour and accessibility are preserved; lint and typecheck pass for the changed area.

title: Redesign blog article page
description: Refine the public blog article page while keeping prose readability as the priority. Improve the article header, metadata, summary callout, related articles, and optional long-post navigation. Consider HeroUI Pro FloatingToc for long articles and ItemCard or ItemCardGroup for related posts.
acceptance_criteria: Article pages have a cleaner header, metadata treatment, and summary callout; related posts use the target design direction; long-form readability is preserved; FloatingToc is evaluated or implemented where appropriate.

title: Redesign blog index with HeroUI Pro
description: Redesign the public /blog index around text-first editorial discovery. The page should prioritise the featured post, series, latest writing, and lightweight search/filtering. Use HeroUI Pro components only where they improve discovery and navigation, such as ItemCard, ItemCardGroup, Segment, Command, Sheet, and EmptyState. Avoid dashboard-like UI.
acceptance_criteria: The blog index has a refreshed editorial composition; discovery and filtering are improved; HeroUI Pro components are used selectively; the page remains readable on desktop and mobile; dashboard-style components are not introduced.

title: Remove shadcn/ui completely
description: Remove shadcn/ui from the project after all usages have been migrated to HeroUI or HeroUI Pro. This is the final cleanup step and should only be started when no @/components/ui/* imports remain.
acceptance_criteria: No @/components/ui/* imports remain; src/components/ui is removed; components.json is removed; shadcn CLI and unused shadcn-only dependencies are removed; lint, typecheck, tests, and build pass.

title: Audit remaining shadcn/ui usage
description: Audit all remaining shadcn/ui usage outside the blog after the blog migration path is established. Group remaining usage by area, including Studio, auth, dashboard, about, projects, and shared home components. The output should guide follow-up migration issues without implementing the replacements yet.
acceptance_criteria: All remaining @/components/ui/* imports are identified; usage is grouped by application area; follow-up migration scope is clear; no component migration is performed as part of this audit unless explicitly requested later.

title: Future: hybrid usage ingestion via Vercel Workflow
description: KIV. Optional enhancement to usage ingestion: local CLI parses ~/.claude + ~/.codex (must stay local), then POSTs daily aggregates to an authed API route that start()s a Vercel Workflow to durably price -> upsert -> revalidate the /usage cache. Adds retries/observability on the cloud half. Parsing cannot move to the cloud (Workflow steps run on Vercel, no local fs). Blocked-by/after the local CLI ingestion (blog-4yt).

title: Integrate WakaTime AI activity stats
description:
## Context

WakaTime has an AI Overview dashboard showing AI-related coding metrics such as AI-attributed lines, AI vs human changes, prompt counts, token usage, and follow-up rates. We want to KIV integrating a documented subset of these stats into the site dashboard.

## Scope

Investigate and implement WakaTime AI activity stats using documented APIs only. The documented WakaTime API appears to expose AI activity indirectly through heartbeats at GET /api/v1/users/current/heartbeats?date=YYYY-MM-DD. Heartbeats may include category: "ai coding", line_additions, and line_deletions.

## Supported Metrics

This can support AI-attributed lines, human-attributed lines, AI additions/deletions, human additions/deletions, and an AI vs Human by day chart.

## Out Of Scope

Input tokens, output tokens, AI prompts, average prompt length, human follow-up rate, and human follow-up edits are excluded unless WakaTime exposes an official documented API for them.

## Implementation Direction

Add a server-only WakaTime client in src/lib/wakatime.ts. Add WAKATIME_API_KEY to environment configuration. Aggregate heartbeats over a configurable date range. Display summary cards in /dashboard. Optionally add a Recharts bar chart for AI vs Human by day. Avoid undocumented dashboard or private endpoints.
acceptance_criteria:
WakaTime API key is never exposed client-side.
Missing API key fails gracefully.
Dashboard can show AI vs human line-change stats when data is available.
Token and prompt metrics are excluded unless WakaTime exposes an official API.
Beads issue is linked or synced with the GitHub issue.
